what: Modeling convention: the Column kernels and per-tick ordering
title: razer ‚Äî project notes for Claude
text: Models composed Column-based per-tick kernels Column buffers (allocate_scalar / allocate_vector). Agent state u8; per-agent timer u16 everywhere (maternal / immunity periods exceed u8‚Äôs 255). Kernels mutate per-agent arrays RETURN per-node counts; model applies deltas census maintains. kernel takes census/flow buffers, model allocates states . flow : carry_forward(_states) copies census column t ‚Üí t+1, model applies kernel‚Äôs returned counts column t+1 move_count(, , counts, t) (/may NULL one-sided moves ‚Äî death decrements , birth increments ). calc_foi(, N, beta, seasonality, network, foi, t) ‚Äî per-node force infection (writes foi report Column; one kernel still reads/writes Columns). transmission(state, timer, nodeid, count, foi, t, to_state, duration) ‚Üí counts ‚Äî S‚Üíto_state (E ), sets u16 timer; returns new infections per node. transmission_si(state, nodeid, count, foi, t) ‚Üí counts ‚Äî S‚Üíabsorbing (timer); SI model. step_si(‚Ä¶, inf_dur) ‚Üí list(waned, onset) ‚Äî M‚ÜíS, E‚Üí. step_sir(‚Ä¶, inf_dur, absorbing_state) ‚Üí list(waned, onset, cleared) ‚Äî adds ‚Üíabsorbing_state (S R). step_sirs(‚Ä¶, inf_dur, imm_dur) ‚Üí list(waned, onset, recovered, waned_r) ‚Äî adds ‚ÜíR (sets immunity timer) R‚ÜíS. births(‚Ä¶) ‚Üí list(count, born), mortality(‚Ä¶) ‚Üí list(m, s, e, , r), constant_pop_vitals_sir(‚Ä¶) (constant-pop convenience; still writes census), import_infections(‚Ä¶). eight-model menagerie ‚Äî every model transmission + step kernel: step kernel single pass branching agent‚Äôs entry state (leads M‚ÜíS, model can add maternal state), just-entered timed state never decremented tick ‚Äî residency artifact (duration ‚àí 1) avoided structurally. One ordering every model ‚Äî always realize full beta ¬∑ D, never beta ¬∑ (D ‚àí 1). tick runs, every model: carry_forward(_states) ‚Üí step_* (apply counts) ‚Üí calc_foi ‚Üí transmission (apply counts) calc_foi placed immediately transmission step kernel . works calc_foi reads settled start--interval infectious census [t] (working column t+1 tick‚Äôs step transmission build), value depend step kernel runs. agent enters one census column infected (via either step kernel‚Äôs E‚Üítransmission‚Äôs S‚Üí) recovers D columns later, contributes FOI exactly D columns occupies ‚Äî R0 = beta ¬∑ D direct S‚ÜíE-entry, special-casing residency artifact. (Historical note: calc_foi used read [t+1], forced different ordering per family; reading [t] removed footgun.) Validated examples/sir_attack_fraction.R / seir_attack_fraction.R (match Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick final size R0 = beta ¬∑ D). See examples/engwal_measles.R full M-S-E--R model simple_sir.R / endemic_sir.R spatial Column SIR. Higher-level helpers. run_model(scenario, model, ‚Ä¶) wires whole menagerie correct order returns model environment bundling $people, $nodes, $network, $carry; seeds states model (E//R column scenario supplies), records per-node flows incidence / onset / recovery / waning applicable, takes optional init(model) plus three per-tick callbacks step_enter (start) / step_update (step kernel calc_foi) / step_exit (end tick) user extension. squash(people) compacts dead agents ; calc_capacity(...) bounds cumulative-births capacity (reclaim) calc_capacity_cdr(...) bounds peak-living capacity squash-reclaimed long runs. binning family bincount() / bincount_wt() (weighted) / bincount_where() (predicate- filtered, count-aware) / bincount_where_wt() (weighted + filtered).

code: carry_forward ‚Üí step (recover I‚ÜíR) ‚Üí calc_foi ‚Üí transmission (infect S‚ÜíI) N <- 1e5L; D <- 10L m <- run_model(data.frame(population = N, I = 100L), "SIR", nticks = 160L,                r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = D, seed = 1L)   # beta = 2.5 / 10 = 0.25 traj <- cbind(S = m$nodes$S$values()[, 1], I = m$nodes$I$values()[, 1], R = m$nodes$R$values()[, 1]) matplot(0:159, traj, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = c("#2c7fb8", "#d7301f", "#238b45"),         xlab = "day", ylab = "agents", main = sprintf("SIR, R0 = beta*D = %.1f", 2.5)) legend("right", c("S", "I", "R"), col = c("#2c7fb8", "#d7301f", "#238b45"), lwd = 2, bty = "n") cat(sprintf("attack fraction here = 1 - S_inf/N = %.3f  (K-M predicts ~0.89)\n",             1 - tail(traj[, "S"], 1) / N)) ## attack fraction here = 1 - S_inf/N = 0.892  (K-M predicts ~0.89)

title: Epidemic final size: R‚ÇÄ, the attack fraction, and Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick
text: sweep R0R_0, run completion, read =1‚àíS‚àû/NA = 1 - S_\infty/N. (Agent-based run_model early stop, just use horizon long enough epidemic finish.) overlay Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick curve.  agreement thousandths across supercritical range ‚Äî strong validation agent model realizes R0=Œ≤DR_0 = \beta D. Just threshold (R0‚âà1R_0 \approx 1) finite seeded population gives small positive attack fraction deterministic theory predicts zero: expected near-critical, finite-size stochastic behavior.
code: km_attack <- function(R0) if (R0 <= 1) 0 else   uniroot(function(A) 1 - exp(-R0 * A) - A, c(1e-9, 1 - 1e-12))$root  sim_attack <- function(R0, n = 1e5L, D = 10L, horizon = 1500L) {   mm <- run_model(data.frame(population = n, I = 50L), "SIR", nticks = horizon,                   r0 = R0, infectious_period = D, seed = 1L)   S <- mm$nodes$S$values()[, 1]; 1 - S[length(S)] / n }  R0_grid <- seq(0.5, 4, by = 0.25) sim <- vapply(R0_grid, sim_attack, numeric(1)) km  <- vapply(R0_grid, km_attack,  numeric(1))  plot(R0_grid, km, type = "l", lwd = 2.5, ylim = c(0, 1),      xlab = expression(R[0]), ylab = "attack fraction",      main = "razer SIR (points) vs Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick (line)") points(R0_grid, sim, pch = 19, col = "#d7301f"); abline(v = 1, lty = 3, col = "grey") cat(sprintf("max |sim - theory| for R0 >= 1.5: %.4f\n", max(abs(sim - km)[R0_grid >= 1.5]))) ## max |sim - theory| for R0 >= 1.5: 0.0035

what: SEIR: a latent stage delays but does not change the final size
title: Epidemic final size: R‚ÇÄ, the attack fraction, and Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick
text: Adding Exposed (latent) state ‚Äî run_model(model = "SEIR", incubation_period = ‚Ä¶) ‚Äî inserts non-infectious delay S‚ÜíE‚Üí‚ÜíRS \E \\R. slows epidemic‚Äôs timing , latent period doesn‚Äôt change many people one case infects, leaves final size obeying relation R0=Œ≤DR_0 = \beta D (infectious period alone).
code: sim_attack_seir <- function(R0, n = 1e5L, D = 10L, De = 5L, horizon = 2000L) {   mm <- run_model(data.frame(population = n, I = 50L), "SEIR", nticks = horizon, r0 = R0,                   infectious_period = D, incubation_period = De, seed = 1L)   S <- mm$nodes$S$values()[, 1]; 1 - S[length(S)] / n } sim_se <- vapply(R0_grid, sim_attack_seir, numeric(1)) plot(R0_grid, km, type = "l", lwd = 2.5, ylim = c(0, 1), xlab = expression(R[0]),      ylab = "attack fraction", main = "SEIR matches the same final-size curve") points(R0_grid, sim_se, pch = 17, col = "#7570b3"); abline(v = 1, lty = 3, col = "grey") legend("bottomright", c("Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick", "razer SEIR"), pch = c(NA, 17),        lwd = c(2.5, NA), col = c("black", "#7570b3"), bty = "n")

what: Ages from a population pyramid (the alias method)
title: Realistic populations: age pyramids and dates of death
text: population pyramid count people age band (often sex). sample million agents‚Äô ages proportion , razer builds AliasedDistribution ‚Äî Walker‚Äôs alias method, draws arbitrary discrete distribution O(1) per sample O(k) setup, far faster cumulative-sum search. sample_pyramid_ages() wraps : pick band proportion population, uniform day within band‚Äôs year range, returning ages days.
code: pyramid    <- load_pyramid_csv(file.path("..", "..", "examples", "data", "pyramid_example.csv"))  # "Age,M,F" bands n_agents   <- 1000000L ages_days  <- sample_pyramid_ages(pyramid, n_agents) ages_years <- ages_days / 365 cat(sprintf("%s agents; ages %.1f‚Äì%.1f yr (mean %.1f)\n",             format(n_agents, big.mark = ","), min(ages_years), max(ages_years), mean(ages_years))) ## 1,000,000 agents; ages 0.0‚Äì101.0 yr (mean 32.8)

what: A date of death (Kaplan‚ÄìMeier, conditioned on current age)
title: Realistic populations: age pyramids and dates of death
text: want agent‚Äôs age death. razer‚Äôs KaplanMeierEstimator takes life table ‚Äî cumulative deaths year synthetic cohort ‚Äî samples age death agent conditioned age alive now, draw never past (essence Kaplan‚ÄìMeier conditional survival). synthesize life table Gompertz‚ÄìMakeham per-year mortality hazard q()q() ‚Äî small baseline, exponential rise age, elevated infant rate ‚Äî running cohort .
code: n_years <- 106L; age <- 0:(n_years - 1L) q <- pmin(0.0004 + 1e-5 * exp(0.115 * age), 1); q[1] <- 0.02      # hazard; q[1] = infant mortality deaths <- numeric(n_years); alive <- 1e5 for (a in seq_len(n_years)) {                                    # run a cohort through the hazard   d <- if (a == n_years) alive else round(alive * q[a])          # force the last year to clear   deaths[a] <- d; alive <- alive - d } km <- kaplan_meier_estimator(cumsum(deaths))                     # non-decreasing cumulative deaths  death_age_days <- km$predict_age_at_death(ages_days, -1L)        # -1L: use the table's last year death_age_years <- death_age_days / 365 stopifnot(all(death_age_days >= ages_days))                      # nobody dies in the past e0 <- mean(km$predict_age_at_death(integer(1e5L), -1L)) / 365    # life expectancy at birth cat(sprintf("predicted age at death: mean %.1f yr; implied e0 = %.1f yr; mean remaining life %.1f yr\n",             mean(death_age_years), e0, mean(death_age_years - ages_years))) ## predicted age at death: mean 77.0 yr; implied e0 = 73.9 yr; mean remaining life 44.2 yr par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)); br <- seq(0, n_years, 1) hist(ages_years, breaks = br, col = "steelblue", border = "white",      xlab = "age (years)", ylab = "agents", main = "Sampled age structure (the pyramid, realized)") hist(death_age_years, breaks = br, col = "firebrick", border = "white",      xlab = "age at death (years)", ylab = "agents",      main = "Predicted age at death (note the infant spike and the old-age bulk)") abline(v = e0, lty = 2, col = "grey30"); legend("topleft", sprintf("e0 = %.1f yr", e0), lty = 2, bty = "n")

what: From a single epidemic to a persistent one
title: Endemic dynamics: vital turnover, importation, and seasonality
text: final-size notebook showed closed SIR burning susceptibles stopping. Real endemic diseases persist susceptible pool continually replenished ‚Äî births, loss immunity, migration. Add demographic turnover (birth/death rate Œº\mu) SIR system non-trivial endemic equilibrium: S*N=1R0,*N=ŒºŒ≤(R0‚àí1).\frac{S^*}{N} = \frac{1}{R_0}, \qquad \frac{^*}{N} = \frac{\mu}{\beta}\,(R_0 - 1). susceptible fraction settles 1/R01/R_0 ‚Äî herd-immunity threshold ‚Äî exactly level effective reproduction number Reff=R0S/NR_\text{eff} = R_0\,S/N equals 1: case replaces , . Push SS 1/R01/R_0 epidemic grows; , shrinks. approach S*S^* series damped epidemic waves.

title: Endemic dynamics: vital turnover, importation, and seasonality
text: run_model() closed-population (allocates exactly initial agents). Vital dynamics importation layered callbacks capacity argument: capacity reserves agent slots initial count, import_infections() somewhere put newly-introduced cases (closed run grow). step_exit callback runs tick transmission: constant_pop_vitals_sir applies births = deaths (death reborn susceptible, NN constant), import_infections seeds cases schedule. importation? finite stochastic population can last infectious agent recover chance ‚Äî fade-‚Äî critical community size. trickle imports keeps chain alive observe equilibrium rather extinction. First, factor constant-population vitals + importation reusable pair callbacks (use runs ). watch system approach equilibrium rather land answer, seed population S*S^* ‚Äî half susceptible, well 1/R0‚âà0.331/R_0 \approx 0.33 ‚Äî let relax.  Starting S/N=0.5S/N = 0.5, first epidemic overshoots ‚Äî driving susceptibles well 1/R01/R_0 ‚Äî births refill pool, smaller epidemic fires, system spirals S*=N/R0S^* = N/R_0 (dotted line) damped waves, persisting low endemic level instead going extinct. (Seed 1/R01/R_0 line flat tick 0 ‚Äî system already rest, endemic_sir.R study equilibrium seasonal response directly.)
code: n_nodes <- 2L; pop <- 5e5L; R0 <- 3; cdr <- 20                  # crude death rate (per 1,000/yr) network <- matrix(c(0, 0.01, 0.01, 0), n_nodes, byrow = TRUE)   # 1% cross-coupling  # init + step_exit for an endemic SIR: constant-population births = deaths (each death reborn # susceptible, so N is constant) plus a fixed importation schedule that prevents fade-out. endemic_callbacks <- function(nticks, sched) {   st <- as.integer(sched$tick); sn <- as.integer(sched$node); sc <- as.integer(sched$count)   list(     init = function(model) {       model$nodes$death_rate   <- values_map(cdr / 1000 / 365, nticks, model$nodes$count)       model$nodes$births       <- allocate_vector("i32", nticks - 1L, model$nodes$count)       model$nodes$deaths       <- allocate_vector("i32", nticks - 1L, model$nodes$count)       model$nodes$importations <- allocate_vector("i32", nticks - 1L, model$nodes$count)     },     step_exit = function(model) {       constant_pop_vitals_sir(model$people$state, model$people$timer, model$people$nodeid,         model$people$count, model$nodes$death_rate, model$nodes$S, model$nodes$I,         model$nodes$R, model$nodes$births, model$nodes$deaths, model$tick)       model$people$count <- import_infections(model$people$state, model$people$timer,         model$people$nodeid, model$people$count, model$nodes$I, model$nodes$importations,         st, sn, sc, dist_gamma(2, 4), model$tick)     }) } nticks   <- 40L * 365L                                              # long enough to settle sched    <- expand.grid(tick = seq(0L, nticks - 2L, 30L), node = 0:1); sched$count <- 5L # Seed ABOVE the equilibrium: half the population susceptible (R = N/2 immune), so S0 = 0.5 > 1/R0. scenario <- data.frame(population = rep(pop, n_nodes), I = rep(100L, n_nodes),                        R = rep(round(0.5 * pop), n_nodes)) cb <- endemic_callbacks(nticks, sched) m <- run_model(scenario, "SIR", nticks = nticks, r0 = R0, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4),                network = network, capacity = sum(scenario$population) + sum(sched$count),                seed = 1L, init = cb$init, step_exit = cb$step_exit)  yr <- (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365 S  <- rowSums(m$nodes$S$values()) / (n_nodes * pop)                 # susceptible FRACTION I  <- rowSums(m$nodes$I$values()) par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) plot(yr, S, type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#2c7fb8", ylim = c(0, max(S) * 1.05),      xlab = "year", ylab = "susceptible fraction", main = "Susceptibles overshoot, then damp to S* = 1/R0") abline(h = 1 / R0, lty = 3, col = "#2c7fb8")                        # the equilibrium S*/N = 1/R0 plot(yr, I / 1e3, type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#d7301f",      xlab = "year", ylab = "infectious (thousands)", main = "Damped epidemic waves to an endemic level")

what: Seasonal forcing: a small wobble, a big response
title: Endemic dynamics: vital turnover, importation, and seasonality
text: Make transmission seasonal passing run_model time-varying seasonality multiplier (values_map-broadcastable shape). endemic system sits critically poised Reff‚âà1R_\text{eff}\approx 1, even gentle annual sinusoid (¬±10%\pm 10\% Œ≤\beta) drives pronounced, phase-locked annual epidemic waves ‚Äî resonance phenomenon central dynamics childhood infections like measles. seed equilibrium forced waves stand without startup transient.
code: nticks   <- 10L * 365L sched    <- expand.grid(tick = seq(0L, nticks - 2L, 30L), node = 0:1); sched$count <- 10L scenario <- data.frame(population = rep(pop, n_nodes), I = rep(100L, n_nodes),                        R = rep(pop - round(pop / R0), n_nodes))      # seed S = N/R0 (at equilibrium) amp  <- 0.10 seas <- 1 + amp * sin(2 * pi * (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365)        # length-nticks per-day multiplier cb <- endemic_callbacks(nticks, sched) ms <- run_model(scenario, "SIR", nticks = nticks, r0 = R0, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4),                 network = network, seasonality = seas,                 capacity = sum(scenario$population) + sum(sched$count),                 seed = 1L, init = cb$init, step_exit = cb$step_exit) yr <- (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365 par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) plot(yr, seas, type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#e6a000", xlab = "year", ylab = "beta multiplier",      main = sprintf("Seasonal forcing (+/-%.0f%%)", 100 * amp)); abline(h = 1, lty = 3, col = "grey") plot(yr, rowSums(ms$nodes$I$values()) / 1e3, type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#d7301f",      xlab = "year", ylab = "infectious (thousands)", main = "Phase-locked annual epidemic waves") abline(v = 0:10, col = "grey92")

title: Endemic dynamics: vital turnover, importation, and seasonality
text: Mortality / turnover. Raise cdr (20/1000/yr) replenish susceptibles faster ‚Äî larger Œº\mu raises endemic *^* shortens inter-epidemic period. Importation. Edit sched data frame (ticks, nodes, many) ‚Äî set zero watch small population fade (cross critical community size). Forcing. code: scenario <- data.frame(population = 1e5L, I = 100L) m <- run_model(scenario, "SEIR", nticks = 200L, r0 = 2.5,                infectious_period = 8, incubation_period = 5, seed = 1L)  # $values() copies a census Column back as an (nticks x n_nodes) matrix; [, 1] is the node. traj <- cbind(S = m$nodes$S$values()[, 1], E = m$nodes$E$values()[, 1],               I = m$nodes$I$values()[, 1], R = m$nodes$R$values()[, 1]) matplot(0:199, traj, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2,         col = c("#2c7fb8", "#ff7f00", "#e31a1c", "#33a02c"),         xlab = "day", ylab = "agents", main = "SEIR epidemic") legend("right", c("S", "E", "I", "R"), col = c("#2c7fb8", "#ff7f00", "#e31a1c", "#33a02c"),        lwd = 2, bty = "n")

code: N <- 1e6L; nticks <- 365L; D <- dist_gamma(140, 0.05)   # mean 7-day periods runs <- lapply(c("SIR", "SIRS", "SEIR", "SEIRS"), function(mod) {   args <- list(scenario = data.frame(population = N, I = 100L), model = mod,                nticks = nticks, r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = D, seed = 1L)   if (grepl("E", mod))            args$incubation_period <- D   if (mod %in% c("SIRS", "SEIRS")) args$immunity_period  <- 60   do.call(run_model, args) }) names(runs) <- c("SIR", "SIRS", "SEIR", "SEIRS")  col_comp  <- c(S = "#1f78b4", E = "#ff7f00", I = "#e31a1c", R = "#33a02c") lty_model <- c(SIR = 1, SIRS = 2, SEIR = 3, SEIRS = 5) plot(NA, xlim = c(0, nticks - 1), ylim = c(0, 1), xlab = "day", ylab = "agents (millions)",      main = "SIR / SIRS / SEIR / SEIRS") for (mod in names(runs)) for (cmp in c("S", "E", "I", "R")) {   col <- runs[[mod]]$nodes[[cmp]]   if (!is.null(col)) lines(0:(nticks - 1), rowSums(col$values()) / 1e6,                            col = col_comp[cmp], lty = lty_model[mod], lwd = 1.8) } legend("top", names(col_comp), col = col_comp, lwd = 2, lty = 1, horiz = TRUE, bty = "n") legend("right", names(lty_model), col = "grey30", lwd = 2, lty = lty_model, bty = "n")

title: razer ‚Äî getting started (R Notebook)
text: run_model() takes init / step_enter / step_update / step_exit callbacks extra_states argument user-defined states. one-vaccination moves 40% still-susceptible population new "V" state day 10 ‚Äî early, wave takes ‚Äî something prevent (see examples/sia_campaigns.R examples/quarantine.R scheduled / leaky versions). compare SIR model without pulse, difference intervention alone.
code: sir_args <- list(scenario, "SIR", nticks = 200L, r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = 8, seed = 1L) base <- do.call(run_model, sir_args)                       # no intervention vacc <- do.call(run_model, c(sir_args, list(extra_states = "V",                   step_exit = function(model) {                     if (model$tick != 10L) return(invisible())                     V <- model$states[["V"]]; S <- model$states[["S"]]                     s <- model$people$state$values()                     take <- which(s == S); take <- take[runif(length(take)) < 0.4]                     s[take] <- V; model$people$state$set(s)                     move_count(model$nodes$S, model$nodes$V, length(take), model$tick)                   }))) matplot(0:199, cbind(`no intervention` = rowSums(base$nodes$I$values()),                      vaccinated = rowSums(vacc$nodes$I$values())) / 1e3,         type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = c("#e31a1c", "#1f78b4"),         xlab = "day", ylab = "infectious (thousands)",         main = "Same SIR, with vs without a day-10 vaccination pulse") legend("topright", c("no intervention", "day-10 pulse (40% of S -> V)"),        col = c("#e31a1c", "#1f78b4"), lwd = 2, bty = "n")

title: Interventions: custom states, vaccination campaigns, and quarantine
text: Supplemental Immunization Activity: schedule, targeted nodes, move susceptible new "V" state probability coverage. V protected ‚Äî force infection reads census, disease step never touches V. Three independent patches, two targeted one control, make node-targeting visible.
code: n_nodes <- 3L; pop <- 3e5L; nticks <- 3L * 365L campaign_ticks <- seq(30L, nticks - 2L, by = 365L)   # annual, first one early (preventive) affected <- c(0L, 1L); coverage <- 0.6  campaign <- function(model) {   if (!(model$tick %in% campaign_ticks)) return(invisible())   V <- model$states[["V"]]; S <- model$states[["S"]]   s <- model$people$state$values(); nid <- model$people$nodeid$values()   cnt <- model$people$count   elig <- which(s[seq_len(cnt)] == S & nid[seq_len(cnt)] %in% affected)   vacc <- elig[runif(length(elig)) < coverage]                # probabilistic coverage   if (!length(vacc)) return(invisible())   s[vacc] <- V; model$people$state$set(s)   move_count(model$nodes$S, model$nodes$V, tabulate(nid[vacc] + 1L, model$nodes$count), model$tick) }  vax <- run_model(data.frame(population = rep(pop, n_nodes), I = rep(50L, n_nodes)),                  "SIR", nticks = nticks, r0 = 2, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4),                  network = matrix(0, n_nodes, n_nodes), seed = 1L,                  extra_states = "V", step_exit = campaign)  yr <- (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365; col <- c("#1b9e77", "#7570b3", "#d95f02") par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) matplot(yr, vax$nodes$V$values() / 1e3, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = col,         xlab = "year", ylab = "vaccinated (000s)", main = "V staircases up in the two targeted nodes") legend("topleft", c("node 0 (targeted)", "node 1 (targeted)", "node 2 (control)"), col = col, lwd = 2, bty = "n") matplot(yr, vax$nodes$I$values() / 1e3, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = col,         xlab = "year", ylab = "infectious (000s)", main = "...and only the control burns through")

what: 2. Add waning immunity (V ‚Üí S) ‚Äî one extra callback
title: Interventions: custom states, vaccination campaigns, and quarantine
text: Permanent immunity just absence return transition. make V wane, set timer vaccinating add step_update runs step_timer_expire(V ‚Üí S) tick. ‚Äôs entire change ‚Äî new kernel, disease-step edit.  Permanent V staircased ; waning V sawtooths ‚Äî campaign code, plus one timer one step_update. composability point.
code: waning_days <- dist_normal(2 * 365, 90)   # ~2-year immunity, per-agent draw  campaign_timed <- function(model) {        # as before, but also set the waning timer   if (!(model$tick %in% campaign_ticks)) return(invisible())   V <- model$states[["V"]]; S <- model$states[["S"]]   s <- model$people$state$values(); nid <- model$people$nodeid$values(); tm <- model$people$timer$values()   cnt <- model$people$count   elig <- which(s[seq_len(cnt)] == S & nid[seq_len(cnt)] %in% affected)   vacc <- elig[runif(length(elig)) < coverage]   if (!length(vacc)) return(invisible())   s[vacc] <- V; tm[vacc] <- pmax(1L, round(waning_days$sample_n(length(vacc))))   model$people$state$set(s); model$people$timer$set(tm)   move_count(model$nodes$S, model$nodes$V, tabulate(nid[vacc] + 1L, model$nodes$count), model$tick) } wane <- function(model) {                  # the new piece: V -> S on timer expiry   V <- model$states[["V"]]; S <- model$states[["S"]]   w <- step_timer_expire(model$people$state, model$people$timer, model$people$nodeid,                          model$people$count, model$nodes$count, V, S)   move_count(model$nodes$V, model$nodes$S, w, model$tick) } vaxw <- run_model(data.frame(population = rep(pop, n_nodes), I = rep(50L, n_nodes)),                   "SIR", nticks = nticks, r0 = 2, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4),                   network = matrix(0, n_nodes, n_nodes), seed = 1L, extra_states = "V",                   step_update = wane, step_exit = campaign_timed) par(mfrow = c(1, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) matplot(yr, vaxw$nodes$V$values() / 1e3, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = col,         xlab = "year", ylab = "vaccinated (000s)",         main = "With waning, V sawtooths (campaign jumps, then decay) ‚Äî campaigns must repeat") legend("topleft", c("node 0", "node 1", "node 2 (control)"), col = col, lwd = 2, bty = "n")

what: 3. Quarantine (a ‚ÄúQ‚Äù state with a different timed transition)
title: Interventions: custom states, vaccination campaigns, and quarantine
text: recipe gives case isolation. periodic, leaky testing programme detects infectious agent probability sensitivity moves ‚Üí Q; isolated agents stop transmitting (Q isn‚Äôt census). fixed period step_update releases Q ‚Üí R ‚Äî step_timer_expire targets R instead S.
code: test_ticks <- seq(0L, 199L, by = 14L); sensitivity <- 0.8; iso_days <- 10L detect <- function(model) {   if (!(model$tick %in% test_ticks)) return(invisible())   I <- model$states[["I"]]; Q <- model$states[["Q"]]   s <- model$people$state$values(); nid <- model$people$nodeid$values(); cnt <- model$people$count   inf <- which(s[seq_len(cnt)] == I); det <- inf[runif(length(inf)) < sensitivity]   # 20% missed   if (!length(det)) return(invisible())   s[det] <- Q; tm <- model$people$timer$values(); tm[det] <- iso_days   model$people$state$set(s); model$people$timer$set(tm)   move_count(model$nodes$I, model$nodes$Q, tabulate(nid[det] + 1L, model$nodes$count), model$tick) } release <- function(model) {   Q <- model$states[["Q"]]; R <- model$states[["R"]]   r <- step_timer_expire(model$people$state, model$people$timer, model$people$nodeid,                          model$people$count, model$nodes$count, Q, R)   move_count(model$nodes$Q, model$nodes$R, r, model$tick) } base <- run_model(data.frame(population = 5e5L, I = 100L), "SIR", nticks = 200L,                   r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4), seed = 1L) quar <- run_model(data.frame(population = 5e5L, I = 100L), "SIR", nticks = 200L,                   r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4), seed = 1L,                   extra_states = "Q", step_update = release, step_exit = detect) matplot(0:199, cbind(baseline = rowSums(base$nodes$I$values()),                      quarantine = rowSums(quar$nodes$I$values())) / 1e3,         type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2.5, col = c("#d7301f", "#2c7fb8"),         xlab = "day", ylab = "infectious (000s)",         main = "Fortnightly 80%-sensitive case isolation flattens the curve") legend("topright", c("baseline", "with quarantine"), col = c("#d7301f", "#2c7fb8"), lwd = 2.5, bty = "n")

title: Long runs and memory: capacity and squash()
text: razer agent-based ‚Äî every individual occupies slot fixed-size Column arrays. closed population easy: allocate exactly NN slots. add births, array must hold every agent ever exist. century crude birth rate 30/1,000/year, tens millions slots even though million ever alive . Allocating cumulative total wasteful; fix reclaim slots dead reuse . Two helpers bracket choice: calc_capacity(birthrates, pop) ‚Äî cumulative-births bound: many agents ever born (safety factor). Use never reclaim. calc_capacity_cdr(birthrates, deathrates, pop) ‚Äî peak-living bound: net births minus conservatively -counted death rate. Use reclaim dead slots periodically squash(). squash(people) compacts living agents front every per-agent Column (keeping aligned) drops dead, returning new live count ‚Äî active count tracks living population instead cumulative total.
code: N0 <- 500000L; years <- 100L; nticks <- years * 365L; cbr <- 30; cdr <- 15 br <- matrix(cbr, nticks - 1L, 1L); dr <- matrix(cdr, nticks - 1L, 1L) cap_cdr   <- as.integer(calc_capacity_cdr(br, dr, N0, safety_factor = 1))   # we allocate this cap_naive <- as.integer(min(calc_capacity(br, N0, safety_factor = 1), .Machine$integer.max)) cat(sprintf("calc_capacity_cdr (peak-living, with squash) = %s slots\n", format(cap_cdr, big.mark = ","))) ## calc_capacity_cdr (peak-living, with squash) = 4,564,671 slots cat(sprintf("calc_capacity     (cumulative, no reclaim)   = %s slots  (%.1fx more)\n",             format(cap_naive, big.mark = ","), cap_naive / cap_cdr)) ## calc_capacity     (cumulative, no reclaim)   = 42,128,784 slots  (9.2x more)

title: Long runs and memory: capacity and squash()
text: disease (model "SI" r0 = 0, nothing transmits) ‚Äî just demography. Crude, age-independent mortality exponential lifespan CDR hazard (Kaplan‚ÄìMeier table built exponential survival curve). Newborns enter maternal state M (registered via extra_states; run_model wanes M‚ÜíS us). step_exit callback runs births mortality tick squash()es year, recording living active counts plots.  living population grows steadily, active slot count tracks closely ‚Äî staying well allocated calc_capacity_cdr line far calc_capacity (-reclaim) bound. gap memory squash() saves: without allocate every agent ever born.
code: h_year <- -log(1 - cdr / 1000)                                  # constant per-year hazard km <- kaplan_meier_estimator(round((1 - exp(-h_year * (0:200))) * 1e7))   # exponential life table mat_dur <- dist_normal(270, 20); squash_every <- 365L  track <- new.env() track$live <- numeric(nticks); track$live[1] <- N0 track$active <- numeric(nticks); track$active[1] <- N0  m <- run_model(data.frame(population = N0), "SI", nticks = nticks, r0 = 0,                infectious_period = dist_constant(7),            # unused (r0 = 0)                capacity = cap_cdr, extra_states = "M", seed = 1L,                init = function(model) {                  cap <- model$people$capacity                  model$people$dob <- allocate_scalar("i32", cap)                  model$people$dod <- allocate_scalar("u32", cap)                  model$nodes$birth_rate <- values_map(cbr / 1000 / 365, nticks, 1L)                  age <- pmin(as.integer(floor(stats::rexp(N0, h_year / 365))), 90L * 365L)                  model$people$dob$set(c(-age, integer(cap - N0)))                  model$people$dod$set(c(as.integer(km$predict_age_at_death(age, -1L) - age),                                         integer(cap - N0)))                },                step_exit = function(model) {                  t <- model$tick; idx <- t + 2L                  b <- births(model$people$state, model$people$timer, model$people$nodeid,                              model$people$dob, model$people$dod, model$people$count, 1L,                              model$nodes$birth_rate, mat_dur, km, t)                  model$people$count <- b$count; move_count(NULL, model$nodes$M, b$born, t)                  d <- mortality(model$people$state, model$people$dod, model$people$nodeid,                                 model$people$count, 1L, t)                  move_count(model$nodes$M, NULL, d$m, t); move_count(model$nodes$S, NULL, d$s, t)                  if ((t + 1L) %% squash_every == 0L) model$people$count <- squash(model$people)                  track$live[idx]   <- sum(model$nodes$M$values()[idx, ], model$nodes$S$values()[idx, ])                  track$active[idx] <- model$people$count                }) cat(sprintf("living %s -> %s (%.1fx growth); peak active = %s of %s capacity (%.0f%% used)\n",             format(N0, big.mark = ","), format(round(track$live[nticks]), big.mark = ","),             track$live[nticks] / N0, format(round(max(track$active)), big.mark = ","),             format(cap_cdr, big.mark = ","), 100 * max(track$active) / cap_cdr)) ## living 500,000 -> 1,915,754 (3.8x growth); peak active = 1,947,029 of 4,564,671 capacity (43% used) yr <- (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365 par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) plot(yr, track$live / 1e6, type = "l", lwd = 2.5, col = "#238b45", ylim = c(0, cap_naive / 1e6 * 1.05),      xlab = "year", ylab = "agents (millions)", main = "Living population vs the two capacity bounds") abline(h = cap_cdr / 1e6, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#d7301f") abline(h = cap_naive / 1e6, lty = 3, lwd = 2, col = "grey40") legend("topleft", c("living population", "calc_capacity_cdr (allocated)", "calc_capacity (no reclaim)"),        col = c("#238b45", "#d7301f", "grey40"), lwd = c(2.5, 2, 2), lty = c(1, 2, 3), bty = "n") matplot(yr, cbind(active = track$active, living = track$live) / 1e6, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2,         col = c("#2c7fb8", "#238b45"), ylim = c(0, cap_cdr / 1e6 * 1.05),         xlab = "year", ylab = "agents (millions)", main = "Annual squash() keeps active slots near the living count") abline(h = cap_cdr / 1e6, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#d7301f") legend("topleft", c("active slots (people$count)", "living population", "allocated capacity"),        col = c("#2c7fb8", "#238b45", "#d7301f"), lwd = c(2, 2, 2), lty = c(1, 1, 2), bty = "n")

title: Long runs and memory: capacity and squash()
text: Squash cadence. Squash less often (e.g.¬Ýevery 5 years) active count sawtooths higher reclaims (dead slots accumulate, drop) ‚Äî trading little head-room fewer compactions. Size capacity worst-case inter-squash peak. Safety factor. calc_capacity_cdr(..., safety_factor = s) credits 1/(1+s) deaths, larger s reserves slack lower-mortality (faster-growing) realization; s = 0 bare net-growth bound. Add disease. Swap "SI", r0 = 0 real model incubation_period / transmission endemic, demographically open simulation can run centuries within bounded array (exactly long measles polio run needs).

title: Spatial epidemics: metapopulations and migration networks
text: single well-mixed population useful fiction, real epidemics play across metapopulation ‚Äî set patches (towns, districts, countries) local transmission, coupled movement people. Coupling drives phenomena single-patch model can‚Äôt show: spatial invasion waves, rescue faded-patch neighbours, partial synchrony patches. razer models metapopulation nn patches running usual SIR transmission, plus coupling matrix WW WijW_{ij} fraction patch ii‚Äôs force infection exported patch jj. kernel calc_foi redistributes tick: Œªk=rk(1‚àí‚àëjWkj)+‚àëiriWik,rk=Œ≤kIkNk,\lambda_k \;=\; r_k\Big(1 - \textstyle\sum_j W_{kj}\Big) \;+\; \sum_i r_i\,W_{ik}, \qquad r_k = \beta_k\,\frac{I_k}{N_k}, .e.¬Ýpatch kk keeps un-exported part local force imports share every patch‚Äôs.

title: Spatial epidemics: metapopulations and migration networks
text: use England & Wales measles patches (954 registration districts, 1944 population latitude/longitude). razer ships several migration models turn pairwise distances + populations weight matrix ‚Äî gravity(), radiation(), stouffer(), competing_destinations() ‚Äî plus row_normalizer() cap patch‚Äôs total emigration rows valid export fractions. : parameter-free radiation model (Simini et al., Nature 2012), capped 10% emigration per patch.
code: # A pkgdown article under vignettes/articles/, so the shared example data is two up. scenario <- read.csv(file.path("..", "..", "examples", "data", "EnglandWalesMeasles_places.csv")) D  <- distances(scenario$latitude, scenario$longitude)            # pairwise great-circle km W  <- radiation(scenario$population, D, k = 1, include_home = FALSE) W  <- row_normalizer(W, max_rowsum = 0.1)                         # cap total emigration at 10% cat(sprintf("%d patches; total population %s; network row-sums in [%.3f, %.3f]\n",             nrow(scenario), format(sum(scenario$population), big.mark = ","),             min(rowSums(W)), max(rowSums(W)))) ## 954 patches; total population 30,182,538; network row-sums in [0.009, 0.100]

title: Vital dynamics and maternal immunity: a measles model
text: Measles canonical childhood infection: transmissible (R0‚âà12R_0 \approx 12‚Äì1818), fully immunizing, ‚Äî crucially ‚Äî sustained birth new susceptibles. Modelling well needs SIR: M ‚Äî maternal immunity: newborns temporarily protected maternal antibodies, wane S ~9 months. disease chain M ‚Üí S ‚Üí E ‚Üí ‚Üí R. vital dynamics: crude birth rate feeds newborns M, every agent realistic age date death natural mortality retires schedule. susceptible supply (births) makes system endemic, damped/forced recurrent epidemics familiar pre-vaccination measles.

title: Vital dynamics and maternal immunity: a measles model
text: razer‚Äôs extensibility pays ‚Äî kernel changes, just run_model() knobs: extra_states = "M" registers maternal state. disease kernels lead M‚ÜíS waning leg, run_model applies us tick (recording waning_m flow). capacity (calc_capacity()) reserves slots population grow 20-year run; births kernel activates . init callback gives agent age (pyramid-like curve) Kaplan‚ÄìMeier date death (see demographics notebook). step_exit callback runs births (newborns M, maternal timer + KM date death) mortality (retire agents whose dod arrived) tick.  Susceptibles hover near herd-immunity threshold N/R0N/R_0 (small 1/14 ‚âà 7% reservoir births top ), M thin layer protected newborns, incidence breaks recurrent epidemics birth-fed susceptible pool repeatedly crosses threshold.
code: # A survivorship curve l(a) from a Gompertz‚ÄìMakeham hazard; its complement (cumulative # deaths) is the life table, and l(a) itself is the initial age distribution. Deriving both # from one curve keeps the age structure and mortality mutually consistent. max_age <- 100L; ay <- 0:max_age haz <- pmin(0.0004 + 1e-5 * exp(0.115 * ay), 1); haz[1] <- 0.02 surv <- numeric(max_age + 2L); surv[1] <- 1 for (a in seq_len(max_age + 1L)) surv[a + 1L] <- surv[a] * (1 - haz[a]) surv[max_age + 2L] <- 0 cohort <- 1e6 age_dist <- aliased_distribution(round(surv[1:(max_age + 1L)] * cohort))      # initial ages km       <- kaplan_meier_estimator(round((surv[1] - surv[2:(max_age + 2L)]) * cohort))  # life table r0 <- 14; cbr <- 30; nticks <- 20L * 365L; pop <- 1000000L inf_dur <- dist_normal(7, 1.5); inc_dur <- dist_normal(7, 1.5)     # infectious / latent (days) mat_dur <- dist_normal(270, 20)                                   # maternal-immunity waning (~9 mo) capacity <- as.integer(calc_capacity(matrix(cbr, nticks - 1L, 1L), pop, safety_factor = 1))  # Seed near the herd-immunity threshold: immune fraction 1 - 1/R0, a small infectious spark. scenario <- data.frame(population = pop, R = round((1 - 1 / r0) * pop), I = 100L)  m <- run_model(scenario, "SEIR", nticks = nticks, r0 = r0, infectious_period = inf_dur,                incubation_period = inc_dur, capacity = capacity, extra_states = "M",                seed = 1L, progress = FALSE,                init = function(model) {                  cap <- model$people$capacity                  model$people$dob <- allocate_scalar("i32", cap)                  model$people$dod <- allocate_scalar("u32", cap)                  model$nodes$birth_rate <- values_map(cbr / 1000 / 365, nticks, 1L)                  model$nodes$births     <- allocate_vector("i32", nticks - 1L, 1L)                  model$nodes$deaths     <- allocate_vector("i32", nticks - 1L, 1L)                  age_days <- age_dist$sample_n(pop) * 365L + as.integer(floor(stats::runif(pop) * 365))                  model$people$dob$set(c(-age_days, integer(cap - pop)))                  model$people$dod$set(c(as.integer(km$predict_age_at_death(age_days, -1L) - age_days),                                         integer(cap - pop)))                },                step_exit = function(model) {                  t <- model$tick                  b <- births(model$people$state, model$people$timer, model$people$nodeid,                              model$people$dob, model$people$dod, model$people$count, 1L,                              model$nodes$birth_rate, mat_dur, km, t)                  model$people$count <- b$count                  move_count(NULL, model$nodes$M, b$born, t); model$nodes$births$set_col(t, b$born)                  d <- mortality(model$people$state, model$people$dod, model$people$nodeid,                                 model$people$count, 1L, t)                  move_count(model$nodes$M, NULL, d$m, t); move_count(model$nodes$S, NULL, d$s, t)                  move_count(model$nodes$E, NULL, d$e, t); move_count(model$nodes$I, NULL, d$i, t)                  move_count(model$nodes$R, NULL, d$r, t); model$nodes$deaths$set_col(t, d$m+d$s+d$e+d$i+d$r)                }) cat(sprintf("living %s -> %s over 20 yr; cases %s; births %s; deaths %s\n",             format(pop, big.mark = ","),             format(round(sum(sapply(c("M","S","E","I","R"), function(s) m$nodes[[s]]$values()[nticks, ]))), big.mark = ","),             format(sum(m$nodes$incidence$values()), big.mark = ","),             format(sum(m$nodes$births$values()), big.mark = ","),             format(sum(m$nodes$deaths$values()), big.mark = ","))) ## living 1,000,000 -> 1,430,070 over 20 yr; cases 641,290; births 715,584; deaths 285,514 yr <- (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365 S <- rowSums(m$nodes$S$values()); R <- rowSums(m$nodes$R$values()); M <- rowSums(m$nodes$M$values()) par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) matplot(yr, cbind(S, R, M), type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = c("steelblue", "darkgreen", "purple"),         xlab = "year", ylab = "agents", main = "Immune-status reservoirs: S, R, M") abline(h = pop / r0, lty = 3, col = "steelblue")                 # S* = N / R0 legend("right", c("S", "R", "M", "N/R0"), col = c("steelblue", "darkgreen", "purple", "steelblue"),        lwd = c(2, 2, 2, 1), lty = c(1, 1, 1, 3), bty = "n") plot(yr[-1], rowSums(m$nodes$incidence$values()), type = "l", col = "firebrick",      xlab = "year", ylab = "new infections / day", main = "Daily incidence: recurrent measles epidemics") abline(v = 0:20, col = "grey92")

text: Full models compose per-tick kernels loop; see examples/.
code: library(razer)  # Disease-state codes shared by all kernels: c(S = 0, E = 1, I = 2, R = 3, M = 4, D = -1) states <- laser_states()  # A typed, Rust-owned agent-property array: one million u8 disease states. state  <- allocate_scalar("u8",  1e6)        # per-agent state (1 byte each) nodeid <- allocate_scalar("u16", 1e6)        # 0-based patch id per agent timer  <- allocate_scalar("u8",  1e6)        # state-timer countdown state$set(rep(states[["S"]], 1e6))           # write the whole buffer state$values()[1:5]                          # copy a snapshot back to R  # A per-node, per-tick report buffer (n_ticks x n_nodes), stored time-major so each # tick's per-node row is contiguous. The census is maintained INCREMENTALLY: each tick # carries column t forward to t+1, then kernels apply only their deltas. S <- allocate_vector("i32", 365L, 10L)       # 365 ticks x 10 nodes S$set_col(0L, rep(1e5, 10L))                 # tick 0, per-node susceptibles  # Draw state-timer durations from a parameterized distribution. inf_period <- dist_gamma(2, 4)               # mean 8 ticks inf_period$sample_n(5L)

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Column ‚Äî Rust-owned, dtype-tagged array (i8/u8/i16/u16/i32/u32/f32/f64), allocated 1-D per-agent array (allocate_scalar) 2-D time √ó node report buffer (allocate_vector). $set()/$values()/$col()/$set_col() move data R; kernels operate buffer place. narrow integer widths (e.g.¬Ýu8 state, u16 node id / timer) keep national-scale populations compact. State codes ‚Äî laser_states() returns c(S, E, , R, M, D); M maternal immunity, D deceased (stored 255 u8 state column). Distributions ‚Äî Distribution (dist_normal(), dist_gamma(), dist_constant(), ‚Ä¶) supplies per-agent state-timer durations. Per-tick kernels ‚Äî calc_foi (force infection, spatial coupling); transmission kernels transmission (S‚ÜíE S‚Üí, sets u16 timer) transmission_si (S‚Üíabsorbing); three step kernels step_si / step_sir / step_sirs (timed M‚ÜíS / E‚Üí/ ‚Üí{S,R} / R‚ÜíS transitions SI‚Ä¶SEIRS menagerie, single u16-timer pass); carry_forward / carry_forward_states (incremental census) move_count (apply kernel‚Äôs returned counts); vital-dynamics kernels births, mortality, constant_pop_vitals_sir, import_infections. agent-loop kernels return per-node counts model applies census ‚Äî model allocates states . parallelize per-agent work across cores private per-node accumulators. Demographics & initialization ‚Äî AliasedDistribution (aliased_distribution) sampling ages pyramid, KaplanMeierEstimator (kaplan_meier_estimator) realistic dates death, load_pyramid_csv / sample_pyramid_ages, calc_capacity / calc_capacity_cdr sizing agent arrays births (latter bounds peak living population runs reclaim dead slots squash). Compaction & binning ‚Äî squash reclaims slots departed (e.g.¬Ýdeceased) agents; parallel binning family bincount / bincount_wt (weighted) / bincount_where (predicate-filtered, count-aware) / bincount_where_wt (weighted + filtered) rolls per-agent properties per-node, per-tick reports. Spatial coupling ‚Äî distances plus migration-network models (gravity, radiation, stouffer, competing_destinations, row_normalizer) build coupling matrix calc_foi redistributes force infection .

what: Modeling: per-tick ordering and the effective R0
title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Every model SI/SEI/SIS/SEIS/SIR/SEIR/SIRS/SEIRS menagerie transmission kernel (transmission S‚ÜíE S‚Üí; transmission_si SI‚Äôs absorbing S‚Üí) plus one step kernel (step_si, step_sir absorbing state parameterized S R, step_sirs). step kernel leads M‚ÜíS, model can add maternal state. Every model uses one per-tick ordering: carry_forward_states ‚Üí step_* ‚Üí calc_foi ‚Üí transmission calc_foi placed immediately transmission (step kernel ). calc_foi reads settled start--interval infectious census [t] (working column building), value depend step kernel runs. newly infectious agent enters one census column infection recovers D columns later, contributing force infection exactly D columns ‚Äî effective basic reproduction number full R0 = beta * D (never beta * (D ‚àí 1)), direct-S‚ÜíE-entry families, special-casing. step kernel single pass branching agent‚Äôs entry state, just-entered timed state never decremented tick. See CLAUDE.md full rationale per-model table. closed-population menagerie usually don‚Äôt wire hand: run_model(scenario, model, nticks, r0, ‚Ä¶) builds population, runs correct per-tick loop applies census deltas, returns model environment (bundling $people, $nodes, $network, $carry) per-node census incidence / onset / recovery / waning flows ‚Äî e.g.¬Ýrun_model(data.frame(population = 1e5, = 100), "SEIR", nticks = 200, r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = 8, incubation_period = 5, seed = 1). seeds model‚Äôs E//R states scenario supplies, takes optional init plus per-tick callbacks step_enter (start) / step_update (step kernel calc_foi) / step_exit (end) extension (see examples/model_callbacks.R). hand-wired examples show hood (go beyond menagerie vital dynamics, importation, maternal immunity).

what: Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population. ‚Äî calc_capacity
title: Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population. ‚Äî calc_capacity
text: Projects node's population forward (possibly time-varying) crude birth rate returns per-node capacity preallocate, inflated optional safety factor. births treated geometric growth: daily growth rate lambda = (1 + CBR/1000)^(1/365) - 1 summed time steps exponentiated (exp(sum lambda)), geometric-Brownian-motion-style estimate expected growth. safety factor adds multiple growth's standard-deviation-like term sqrt(exp(sum lambda)) - 1 headroom stochastic variation.

title: Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population. ‚Äî calc_capacity
text: birthrates 2-D nsteps x nnodes numeric matrix crude birth rates (births per 1,000 individuals per year), 2-D razer Column (e.g. values_map()) whose $values() matrix. value must [0, 100]. initial_pop numeric vector length nnodes: initial population per node. Must non-negative. safety_factor Non-negative headroom multiplier [0, 6] (default 1). 0 gives bare expected-growth estimate; larger values reserve slack.

what: Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick. ‚Äî calc_foi
title: Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick. ‚Äî calc_foi
text: Computes frequency-dependent, network-redistributed FOI rate column tick foi. local rate per node r[k] = beta[k] * seasonality[k] * infected[k] / population[k], redistributed foi[k] = r[k] * (1 - sum_j W[k, j]) + sum_i r[] * W[, k]. transmission kernels turn rate per-tick probability 1 - exp(-foi).

title: Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick. ‚Äî calc_foi
text: infected Infectious-count census Column (nodes$), slice_len == n_nodes. population Per-node population census Column (nodes$N); FOI denominator. beta Transmission-coefficient grid (n_ticks x n_nodes, values_map()). seasonality Seasonal-modifier grid (n_ticks x n_nodes, values_map()). network n_nodes x n_nodes coupling weights, column-major. Either R numeric matrix, razer Column n_nodes * n_nodes f64 holding matrix column-major order. Column form avoids re-marshalling matrix R every tick (build , e.g. nc <- allocate_vector("f64", n, n); nc$set(.vector(W))). foi 2-D f64 Column ((n_ticks-1) x n_nodes); column tick overwritten. tick 0-based tick index: reads beta[tick]/seasonality[tick] infected[tick]/population[tick] (start--interval census), writes foi[tick].

title: Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick. ‚Äî calc_foi
text: Index conventions: infected population census buffers read start--interval column tick (settled census recorded tick tick, infectious count population start interval tick ‚Üí tick+1); beta seasonality exogenous modifier grids read interval column tick; result written foi[tick]. Ordering effective infectious period. calc_foi reads SETTLED census column tick ‚Äî working column tick+1 interval's step kernel transmission build ‚Äî result depend step kernel runs. lets every model use ONE ordering: carry_forward ‚Üí step ‚Üí calc_foi ‚Üí transmission, calc_foi placed immediately transmission. agent contributes FOI exactly D census columns occupies (enters column entry+1 via either step kernel's E‚Üítransmission's S‚Üí, recovers D columns later), realized basic reproduction number full R0 = beta * D ‚Äî direct S‚Üí(SIR) SEIR-style entry, beta*(D-1) artifact per-family special-casing.

text: Builds agent population per-node scenario, seeds , advances one eight SI / SEI / SIS / SEIS / SIR / SEIR / SIRS / SEIRS models nticks ticks using Column kernels correct order (realized basic reproduction number full R0 = beta * D). tick runs, uniformly every model, carry_forward ‚Üí step ‚Üí calc_foi ‚Üí transmission, calc_foi placed immediately transmission (step kernel ); run_model applies every census delta .

title: Run a closed-population agent-based model. ‚Äî run_model
code: run_model(   scenario,   model,   nticks,   r0,   infectious_period,   incubation_period = NULL,   immunity_period = NULL,   network = NULL,   seasonality = 1,   seed = NULL,   progress = FALSE,   capacity = NULL,   extra_states = NULL,   init = NULL,   step_enter = NULL,   step_update = NULL,   step_exit = NULL )

title: Run a closed-population agent-based model. ‚Äî run_model
text: scenario data.frame one row per node integer population column. Optional integer E / / R columns seed state per node ‚Äî states model actually (e.g. E column ignored SIR). S remainder. seeded E + + R must exceed node's population. model One "SI", "SEI", "SIS", "SEIS", "SIR", "SEIR", "SIRS", "SEIRS" (case-insensitive). nticks Number recorded daily states (dynamics run nticks - 1 times). r0 Basic reproduction number; beta = r0 / mean(infectious_period). infectious_period Infectious period ticks (Distribution number). incubation_period Latent/exposed period (required SE* models). immunity_period Waning-immunity period (required SIRS / SEIRS). network Optional n_nodes x n_nodes spatial-coupling matrix (default: -zero matrix ‚Äî independent nodes). seasonality Transmission modifier values_map()-broadcastable form (default 1). seed Optional integer seed; supplied, set_seed() called reproducible run. progress TRUE, draw text progress bar. capacity Optional agent-array capacity (>= total initial population, default). Reserve extra slots ‚Äî e.g. calc_capacity() / calc_capacity_cdr() ‚Äî callback can grow population births import_infections (activate reserved slots). default capacity, population closed (growth). extra_states Optional character vector extra agent-state names beyond model's S/E//R. name already laser_states() (e.g. "M") keeps code; NEW name (e.g. "V" vaccinated) assigned next free code, becoming genuine new agent state. gets census Column carried forward, totalled N, seeded tick 0 agents' states; assigned codes exposed model$states. disease kernels branch S/E//R/M, agent NEW state left untouched (infected, transitioned) ‚Äî transitions drive callbacks (move S‚ÜíV vaccinate; waning, set timer run step_timer_expire(V, S) step_update). "M" , run_model additionally applies step kernels' built-M‚ÜíS waning tick (recording waning_m flow). init Optional function(model) called model built loop. Use add per-agent property Columns, add state census Column (append model$carry carried forward), set agent states/timers, etc. initial per-node census (re)derived agents' states init runs, changing agent states init keeps census consistent. step_enter Optional function(model) called start every tick (carry-forward). Receives model environment; model$tick current 0-based interval index. step_update Optional function(model) called every tick step kernel (disease-state progression) calc_foi. calc_foi reads settled start--interval census [t] (slice model$tick), edit FOI drivers ‚Äî model$nodes$beta, model$nodes$seasonality, model$network (read slice t) ‚Äî influence CURRENT tick's force infection. Census edits made land working slice t+1, affect NEXT tick's FOI, one. step_exit Optional function(model) called end every tick (transmission). Receives model environment (model$tick set).

title: razer (development version)
text: Terminology: ‚Äústate‚Äù, ‚Äúcompartment‚Äù. razer agent-based ‚Äî individual carries state (susceptible, exposed, infectious, recovered, vaccinated, quarantined, maternally immune, deceased) ‚Äî compartmental (cohorts tracked count). prose, comments, docstrings, plot labels, user-facing messages now say ‚Äústate‚Äù accordingly. Two run_model() warning strings changed text: ‚Äúmodel X E/R compartment‚Äù ‚Üí ‚ÄúE/R state‚Äù, extra_states error now reads ‚Äúmust repeat model‚Äôs states‚Äù (matching test patterns updated).

title: razer (development version)
text: Examples now play well RStudio. Every example script device-aware ‚Äî Rscript writes PNG examples/output/ , source()d interactive RStudio session draws Plots pane instead (small to_png/open_png/close_png helper). Added examples/notebooks/getting_started.Rmd, R Notebook (R‚Äôs Jupyter analog) walking run_model(), model comparison, callback-based intervention inline plots; ‚ÄúRunning RStudio‚Äù section examples README. Added three annotated teaching notebooks pairing scripts: attack_fraction.Rmd (R0 = Œ≤¬∑D Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick final size), endemic_dynamics.Rmd (S*/N = 1/R0 equilibrium, importation, seasonality), interventions.Rmd (custom states + callbacks: vaccination ¬± waning, quarantine). Completed set spatial_metapopulation.Rmd (migration networks + England & Wales attack-rate map), demographics.Rmd (age pyramids + Kaplan‚ÄìMeier dates death), vital_dynamics_measles.Rmd (births/mortality + maternal M state), long_runs_and_memory.Rmd (calc_capacity vs calc_capacity_cdr + squash()), every example now paired teaching notebook. Richer example visuals: simple_sir.R (previously plotless) now plots national S//R trajectory geographic England & Wales attack-rate map; quarantine.R gained 2√ó2 figure (infectious baseline-vs-quarantine, cumulative cases averted, Q state, per-test detections). quarantine.R enhancements: testing programme now runs configurable schedule (test_period, default fortnightly) leaky ‚Äî infectious agent detected probability sensitivity (default 0.80; 20% false negatives, false positives). Teaching notebooks now pkgdown articles. eight notebooks moved examples/notebooks/*.Rmd vignettes/articles/*.Rmd rendered package website (Articles menu, grouped Foundations / Building richer models / Interventions & scale) https://clorton.github.io/razer/. website-(.Rbuildignored, don‚Äôt run R CMD check); previously committed static .html renders removed favour site. _pkgdown.yml also gained missing step_timer_expire / step_timer_expire_set reference entries. CHANGELOG.md renamed NEWS.md (conventional R name) pkgdown renders site‚Äôs Changelog page; headed development version pkgdown‚Äôs news parser.

title: razer (development version)
text: One per-tick ordering every model ‚Äî calc_foi immediately transmission. calc_foi now reads settled start--interval infectious census [t] instead working column [t+1] (src/rust/src/transmission.rs). value longer depends step kernel runs, every model uses single ordering carry_forward ‚Üí step ‚Üí calc_foi ‚Üí transmission, step kernel calc_foi transmission. infectious agent still contributes FOI exactly D census columns occupies, R0 = beta * D holds direct-S‚ÜíE-entry families per-family special-casing (old dual ordering gone). Re-validated Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick (SIR SEIR attack fractions still match). run_model examples/tests updated single ordering. run_model() now returns model environment takes lifecycle callbacks. bundles $people, $nodes, $network, $carry one environment (returned, passed callbacks), accepts optional init(model) (, loop) plus three per-tick callbacks: step_enter(model) (start tick), step_update(model) (step kernel calc_foi), step_exit(model) (end tick). now seeds model‚Äôs E//R states scenario supplies column (state absent scenario, model, seeded), records per-node flows incidence (models), onset (E‚Üí), recovery (-exit), waning (R‚ÜíS). $nodes$recoveries renamed $nodes$recovery. Binning functions renamed consistency: bincountw ‚Üí bincount_wt, count_by_where ‚Üí bincount_where.

title: razer (development version)
text: bincount_where_wt() ‚Äî weighted, predicate-filtered binning group (src/rust/src/bincount.rs, R/bincount.R). weighted twin bincount_where(): sums per-agent weight per group first count agents matching prop <op> value, one parallel pass. Completes family bincount / bincount_wt / bincount_where / bincount_where_wt. Covered tests/testthat/test-bincount-.R. calc_capacity_cdr() ‚Äî squash-aware capacity estimate (R/calc_capacity.R). mortality-aware companion calc_capacity(): bounds peak living population (net births minus conservatively underestimated death rate) rather cumulative number ever born, [squash()]-reclaimed run can model decades/centuries without one slot per agent ever born. death rate underestimated safety factor (credited 1/(1+safety_factor)). Covered tests/testthat/test-calc-capacity-cdr.R (1e6 100 years, CBR 30 / CDR 15). bincount_where() ‚Äî flexible predicate-filtered agent binning group (src/rust/src/bincount.rs, R/bincount.R). count-aware, filtered bincount: group g 0..n_groups, counts many first count agents group[] == g satisfy prop[] <op> value (op ‚àà eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge), one parallel pass copy property R. Answers queries like ‚Äúexposed node‚Äù (prop = state, op = "eq", value = E) ‚Äú-fives node‚Äù (prop = dob, op = "gt", value = tick - 5*365). Returns integer vector ad-hoc use, writes report Column slice per-tick model loops. Scans live prefix count, stays correct variable-population (post-squash/birth) models. Covered tests/testthat/test-bincount-.R. Agent compaction: Column$squash(keep) + squash(people) helper (src/rust/src/column.rs, R/squash.R). Column$squash(keep) stably compacts 1-D Column elements flagged logical mask, returning kept count; squash(people) applies one mask (default: still-alive, state != D) across every per-agent Column people environment stay row-aligned, updates active count. reclaims slots agents left simulation (e.g.¬Ýdeceased) per-tick kernels stop iterating births can refill slots ‚Äî compaction Column world missing. per-node census aggregate unaffected. Covered tests/testthat/test-squash.R. Generic composable transition kernels step_timer_expire(, ) step_timer_expire_set(, , duration) (src/rust/src/steps.rs). One timed from_state ‚Üí to_state transition (untimed vs timed destination), returning per-node counts ‚Äî building blocks composing models beyond named menagerie (vaccinated V, second infectious stage, ‚Ä¶) R without writing Rust. Covered tests/testthat/test-timer-expire.R, including hand-built SIRS assembled purely generics conserves population. (Also confirmed bincount / bincountw remain Column-native usable Column model ‚Äî tally per-agent property Column per-node census/report slot; model-use test added.) run_model() ‚Äî high-level runner closed-population menagerie (R/run_model.R). One call builds agent population per-node census scenario, seeds , runs eight SI/SEI/SIS/SEIS/SIR/SEIR/SIRS/SEIRS models Column kernels correct order ‚Äî calc_foi placement yields R0 = beta ¬∑ D, every move_count census delta ‚Äî model one call instead ~100 lines hand-wiring, census silently desync agents. Optional spatial network, seasonality, reproducible seed. Returns people/nodes environments (census + incidence/recoveries flows). Validated Kermack‚ÄìMcKendrick (SIR/SEIR attack fractions match ~1e-3); covered tests/testthat/test-run-model.R (eight models, population conservation, reproducibility, input validation). (Hand-wired examples remain models beyond closed-population menagerie ‚Äî vital dynamics, importation, maternal immunity.) Reproducible, seedable RNG (set_seed() / unset_seed()). kernel randomness now flows src/rust/src/rng.rs: set_seed(s) entire razer run deterministic function s order kernel calls, independent CPU/thread count. parallel kernels split agents FIXED-size chunks (one-per-thread) seed chunk deterministically (call_base, chunk_index), result reproducible regardless Rayon schedules work; per-call counter gives kernel invocation (tick) independent stream. Without seed behaviour unchanged (entropy-seeded, random). model RNG SmallRng (xoshiro256++). agent-loop kernels Distribution / AliasedDistribution / KaplanMeierEstimator samplers converted thread_rng. Covered tests/testthat/test-rng.R.

title: razer (development version)
text: Model compositions (SIR/SEIR/SEIRS test helpers examples) now call per-tick transitions downstream-first (timed state : R‚ÜíS, ‚ÜíR, E‚Üí, S‚ÜíE) exposed/infectious periods reflect full configured duration rather shortened one tick. See CLAUDE.md. Distributions return floating-point values; integer rounding state timers now lives step kernels (shared duration_ticks helper rounds nearest tick clamps minimum 1). Distribution longer exposes integer sample_duration. step_transmission_si(people, nodes, beta, inf_dist) ‚Äî inf_duration (sets timer S‚Üí). step_transmission_se(people, nodes, beta, exp_dist) ‚Äî exp_duration (sets E timer S‚ÜíE). step_exposed_ei(people, inf_dist) ‚Äî inf_duration (sets timer E‚Üí). step_infectious_ir(people, imm_dist) ‚Äî imm_duration (sets R waning timer ‚ÜíR; use dist_constant(0) SIR/SEIR R timer never read). Steps transitioning untimed states (step_infectious_is, step_recovered_rs, step_mortality_cdr, step_births_cbr) unchanged. SI/SIR/SEIR/SEIRS test helpers updated wrap durations dist_constant().
